purpose: Theo Agent OS connector map for Nango-backed OAuth/API integrations. This file is planning and policy only; it does not contain provider tokens, client secrets, refresh tokens, or live connection ids.

sources.label: Nango introduction
sources.note: Nango handles auth, credential storage, refresh, validation, functions, syncs, actions, and webhooks.

sources.label: Nango auth guide
sources.note: A Nango connection stores one user's credentials for one external API and keeps them valid over time.

sources.label: Nango get connection credentials
sources.note: Backends should fetch credentials just before use so token refresh is current.

sources.label: Nango Google setup
sources.note: Google requires an owned OAuth app and explicitly configured scopes.

sources.label: Nango GitHub App OAuth
sources.note: GitHub App OAuth is the preferred candidate for fine-grained GitHub permissions.

policy.read_first: Start every connector as read-only status/search/sync, with no provider writes and no model-visible credentials.
policy.drafts_second: Allow draft artifacts only after read paths prove useful; drafts stay inside Glass/Mouth approval gates.
policy.writes_last: Provider writes require explicit operator confirmation, a typed action envelope, and a post-action receipt.
policy.token_boundary: Nango/provider credentials stay in Nango or backend runtime calls. Glass state may expose provider keys, connection labels, statuses, and receipts only.
policy.privacy_boundary: No broad personal-data sync. Use narrow scopes, source pointers, redaction, and per-connector allowlists before ingesting content into OpenBrain or Obsidian.

connectors.label: GitHub
connectors.purpose: Repo status, issues, pull requests, deploy metadata, commit receipts, and safe code-navigation context.
connectors.first_safe_action: Read repo metadata and recent workflow/deploy status for explicitly allowlisted repositories.
connectors.scope_boundary: Prefer GitHub App fine-grained repository permissions; no pushes, issue writes, PR comments, or secret reads in phase 1.
connectors.receipts: Every fetched repo/deploy/issue summary should produce a Glass connector receipt with source URL and timestamp.
connectors.blocked_until: Nango project/provider config exists, GitHub App permissions are reviewed, and allowlisted repos are declared.

connectors.label: Google Calendar
connectors.purpose: Upcoming events, schedule context, and future reminder/draft event workflows.
connectors.first_safe_action: Read next 24-48 hours of calendar events and show summaries in Glass or heartbeat receipts.
connectors.scope_boundary: Calendar read-only first; event creation/update/delete waits for a separate draft+confirm gate.
connectors.receipts: Calendar checks should record time window, calendar label, event count, and redacted titles unless Coach opts in.
connectors.blocked_until: Google OAuth app exists, scopes are narrowed, and private-calendar redaction rules are written.

connectors.label: Gmail
connectors.purpose: Urgent unread email detection, sender/thread context, and later draft replies through OpenMail-style gates.
connectors.first_safe_action: Read unread metadata only from allowlisted labels/categories and surface urgent candidates without body ingestion by default.
connectors.scope_boundary: No send, delete, archive, mark-read, or body-wide sync in phase 1; draft replies require Glass approval and OpenMail receipts.
connectors.receipts: Email receipts should include provider thread id, sender/domain, urgency reason, and whether body text was withheld.
connectors.blocked_until: Label allowlist, retention rule, redaction rule, and draft-only policy are accepted.

connectors.label: Google Drive / Docs
connectors.purpose: Operator-selected docs and files as source material for Obsidian/OpenBrain, with source pointers and no silent broad crawl.
connectors.first_safe_action: List or fetch only explicitly selected files/folders, then create source receipts before any ingest.
connectors.scope_boundary: No whole-drive sync, no shared-drive crawl, and no health/personal docs ingest without explicit selection.
connectors.receipts: Drive receipts should record file id, title, mime type, selected range/folder, ingest target, and privacy label.
connectors.blocked_until: Picker/allowlist UX exists and OpenBrain/Obsidian ingest rules are reviewed.

connectors.label: Notion
connectors.purpose: Optional workspace docs/tasks sync if Coach actually uses Notion for an active project.
connectors.scope_boundary: No page writes or database mutations before GitHub/Calendar/Gmail prove the connector pattern.
connectors.receipts: Notion receipts should capture selected page/database id, action, and write gate state.
connectors.blocked_until: A real Coach workflow requires Notion and the first three connector pilots are stable.

connectors.label: Linear
connectors.purpose: Optional issue/project tracker bridge if Theo OS moves beyond GitHub/TODO.md for work tracking.
connectors.first_safe_action: Read assigned issues and project status for selected workspaces.
connectors.scope_boundary: No issue creation, comments, labels, or status changes before a typed task envelope exists.
connectors.receipts: Linear receipts should record issue id, project id, read/write mode, and linked Glass run id.
connectors.blocked_until: Coach picks Linear as an actual operating surface.
